title: Balancing Scales
description: Equal means equal. You can find a missing amount by thinking about balance, no formulas needed.

scaffolded.title: Equal means equal
scaffolded.body: A scale stays level only when both sides weigh the same. Identical shapes each carry an equal share.

gate.template: Level means both sides weigh the ___.
gate.hint: A scale only sits level when neither side tips, so the two sides must weigh exactly alike.

steps.text: The circles match, so neither is heavier.

gate.template: Matching circles each carry an ___ share.
gate.hint: The circles all match, so no one of them holds more or less, each share is the same size.

steps.text: Split 8 into 2 equal groups to find one circle.

gate.template: Split 8 into 2 groups, so one circle is ___ pounds.
gate.hint: Share the 8 pounds evenly across the 2 circles, then count how much sits in one group.

scaffolded.prompt: Three identical triangles balance 9 pounds. How much does one triangle weigh?
hints: Identical triangles always carry the same weight as each other.
Split the 9 pounds into three equal shares, one for each triangle.
Picture dealing the 9 pounds out across the triangles until each has its share.

feedback.correct: Yes! Three equal triangles split 9, so each is 3 pounds.
incorrect: Not quite. Split 9 into 3 equal groups.
Matching triangles get equal shares. How big is one share of 9?

scaffolded.prompt: Three identical squares balance 6 pounds. Share the 6 one-pound chips equally. How many pounds per square?
hints: A fair split gives every square the very same pile.
Deal the 6 chips out evenly across the three squares.
Hand out one chip per square each round until all 6 are placed.

feedback.correct: Nice! Every square got the same pile, so that's what one weighs.
incorrect: The squares are identical, so each needs the same number of chips.
Keep sharing until all 6 chips sit in equal piles.

scaffolded.title: Keep it fair: change both sides the same
scaffolded.body: A mystery block sits next to a known weight. Take the same off both pans to leave the block alone.

interaction.resultCaption: Take 2 off each side: 9 becomes 7, so the block balances 7 alone.

predict.prompt: Predict: take 2 off each pan and the right pan reads...

scaffolded.prompt: A mystery block plus 1 pound balances 7 pounds. Uncover the block, keep both pans balanced!

followUpMc.prompt: The block stands alone. How much is it worth?

feedback.correct: Exactly! With the extra pound gone, the block balances what's left.
incorrect: After both sides lose a pound, what does the right pan read?
The block matches the weight across from it. Count the right pan.

hints: A scale stays level only when both pans change the same way.
Lift the extra 1 pound off both pans so the block sits alone.
Once the block is alone, read what the right pan still holds.

feedback.correct: Balanced all the way, and the block is uncovered!
incorrect: You changed only one side. Both pans must lose the same amount.
Take the same off each pan, then read what the block balances.

scaffolded.prompt: Two identical boxes plus 2 pounds balance 10 pounds. How much does one box weigh?
hints: Clear the loose weight first, then deal with the matching boxes.
Take the extra 2 pounds off both sides to leave just the two boxes.
Then split whatever the boxes balance into two equal shares.

feedback.correct: Yes! Clear the 2, then split what's left between the two boxes.
incorrect: Take the 2 off both sides first. What's left for the boxes?
The boxes match. After removing 2, share the rest equally.

mastery.prompt: Three squares balance 15 pounds. One square weighs...

feedback.correct: Correct, 15 shared into 3 equal squares is 5 each.

mastery.prompt: Two triangles plus 3 pounds balance 11 pounds. One triangle weighs...

feedback.correct: Yes! Take away 3 from both sides, then share the rest between two triangles.
incorrect: Take away 3 from both sides first, then split what's left by 2.

followUp.prompt: What if the extra weight were 5 pounds instead of 3, still balancing 11? Would one triangle be heavier or lighter?
followUp.why: Taking 5 off both sides leaves only 6 for the two triangles to share, so each triangle is lighter than before.

mastery.prompt: A mystery block plus 5 pounds balances 13 pounds. The mystery block weighs...

feedback.correct: Right, take 5 off both sides and the block stands alone.
incorrect: Remove 5 from both sides. What's left on the mystery side?

followUp.prompt: What if the block plus 5 balanced 15 instead of 13? Would the block be heavier or lighter?
followUp.why: A bigger total with the same 5 taken off both sides leaves more for the block, so it is heavier.
